overall_rationale: This trio hits all three hard setting constraints head-on: Energon is the explicit secondary-resource mechanic, Combine is the Combiner-merging mechanic, and Alt-Mode embodies transformation/morphology to back up the high TDFC density. They occupy distinct mechanical zones — token ramp, graveyard recursion, and same-permanent profile swap — with no overlap or competition for the same cards. Color coverage is excellent and deliberately spread: Energon (URG), Combine (WBG), and Alt-Mode (WUR) together touch all five colors with healthy balance. Complexity is well-mixed: Energon anchors the simple complexity-1 slot while Combine and Alt-Mode add richer complexity-2 decisions, avoiding an all-simple or all-complex slate. Archetype-wise they reinforce one another — Energon fuels both Combine's reassembly costs and Alt-Mode activations, supporting ramp, go-wide-then-merge Combiner, and tempo/transform draft archetypes simultaneously.

selections.name: Energon
selections.reason: Energon directly satisfies the mandatory 'dedicated secondary resource' constraint as the set's named fuel, and anchors the slate's required complexity-1 simple mechanic in URG.

selections.name: Combine
selections.reason: Combine delivers the mandated Combiner 'merge small units into one large entity' constraint as a graveyard-recursion engine in the under-used WBG colors.

selections.name: Alt-Mode
selections.reason: Alt-Mode provides the in-place morphology/transformation flavor for single-faced cards in WUR, complementing the set's TDFC density with a distinct profile-swap shape.
